what_it_does: This file is a descriptive terrain-entry that catalogues and prospectively maps Tim's universal-mechanics vault, summarizing its substrate-environment theory, key primitives, coverage limits, and conceptual relationship to the Company.
observations: Frontmatter marks it as a terrain-entry with register descriptive, status unconfirmed, and coverage of only 11 of 6355 files
It describes an 89M Obsidian vault at /home/tim/universal-mechanics with no git remote
The substrate-environment is framed as the universal structural form any system operating over addressable content presupposes
It lists eight substrate-mechanics as the spine of the theory
Axes are modelled as functors projecting the substrate into coordinate spaces, with multi-orientation pullback as the universal query form
It states verification-as-arithmetic and identifies Rice's theorem as a boundary
It notes that 5225 operational notes files were deliberately skipped as homogeneous records
It asserts the Company is one instantiation of the theory but explicitly not wired to it
It distinguishes read evidence from inferred or unverified claims
standouts: The substrate-environment theory is presented as the conceptual bedrock the Company instantiates
Axes-and-orientations is identified as the literal upstream of invariant-resolution
No-developer principle and authorship-asymmetry as design constraints
conventions: Uses structured frontmatter with type, register, status, coverage, relates-to, themes, concepts, and tags
Quotes source files with section references
Explicitly flags status as unconfirmed and coverage as partial
Separates verified observations from inferred or unverified claims
concerns: Coverage is extremely partial: only 11 of 6355 files were read
Several claims are explicitly marked inferred or not verified
Status is unconfirmed, so the entry's assertions are provisional
No git remote and 89M of local files may be hard to reproduce or verify
The bulk of the file count in notes/ was skipped, so characterization relies on a homogeneity assumption
notes: A high-level, partially-sourced map of Tim's universal-mechanics vault and its conceptual primitives, explicitly marked provisional.
questions: What does the 38-cycle TypeScript build in universal-substrate-system actually implement?
Which of the 11 read files substantiate each specific claim?
How is the claim that the Company is one instantiation justified without references to VECO/creations/docs?
What is the exact relationship between ConceptV and the Company's memory substrate?
Are the 79+ proofs in math-verification formally checked or merely claimed?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose as a descriptive terrain-entry matches the actual content: it catalogues and prospectively maps the universal-mechanics vault.
intention_signals: To establish a conceptual upstream for the Company's design patterns by articulating a universal structural theory and mapping how its primitives prefigure Company mechanisms, while remaining neutral and unconfirmed.
novelty: Treats a personal Obsidian vault as a formal theory with its own registries, mechanics, and verification layer, and frames domain-agnostic substrate operations as the foundation for AI-human co-articulation.
contribution: A structured, source-anchored summary of an upstream conceptual vault, including its primitives, axes, verification stance, and explicit coverage caveats, useful as a prospecting index and lineage note.
summary_for_embedding: Terrain-entry describing Tim's 89M universal-mechanics Obsidian vault, its substrate-environment theory, eight substrate-mechanics, axes-and-orientations, verification-as-arithmetic, and conceptual relation to the Company, with status unconfirmed and partial coverage.
intention_for_embedding: To catalogue and prospect an upstream science/framework vault as a source of primitives and lineage for the Company's design substrates.
